Laurus Labs Limited Reports Earnings Results for the Second Quarter and Six Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Laurus Labs Limited reported earnings results for the second quarter and six months ended September 30, 2025. For the second quarter, the company reported sales was INR 16,534.7 million compared to INR 12,237 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 16,804.3 million compared to INR 12,283 million a year ago. Net income was INR 1,949.7 million compared to INR 198.4 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 3.61 compared to INR 0.37 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 3.61 compared to INR 0.37 a year ago.
For the six months, sales was INR 32,230.4 million compared to INR 24,186.1 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 32,604.3 million compared to INR 24,257.2 million a year ago. Net income was INR 3,579.9 million compared to INR 323.5 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 6.63 compared to INR 0.6 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 6.63 compared to INR 0.6 a year ago.

data.title: EcoVadis Expands Partnership Network with SGS and QIMA
data.description:
EcoVadis announced a significant expansion of its Partnership & Alliance network with the addition of two onsite audit firms, SGS and QIMA. This strategic move directly addresses the growing demand for trusted recommendations for training and auditing services when risks are identified and targeted verification is required during EcoVadis' ESG assessments. New partner accreditation results from a rigorous selection process.

Both SGS and QIMA were identified based on their commitment to excellence, deep expertise, and client-first approach, ensuring EcoVadis' clients receive high-quality, seamless support. Through the EcoVadis Worker Voice solution suite, data flags high-risk areas where human rights breaches may exist. Users can leverage this intelligence to schedule a highly-focused investigation in order to verify and immediate known issues, ultimately guiding targeted improvements.

They can now tap into a network of accredited experts to conduct onsite verification and monitor remediation. This expansion is a core component of EcoVadis' broader company strategy: to create a thriving ecosystem of partners who drive innovation, deliver exceptional value, and accelerate mutual growth through seamless collaboration and cutting-edge integrations. The partnership with SGS and QIMA strengthens this ecosystem by providing a crucial, complementary service, fostering a more complete value chain for joint clients.

data.title: Allegion plc Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Allegion plc reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported sales was USD 1,070.2 million compared to USD 967.1 million a year ago. Net income was USD 188.4 million compared to USD 174.2 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 2.19 compared to USD 2 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 2.18 compared to USD 1.99 a year ago.
For the nine months, sales was USD 3,034.1 million compared to USD 2,826.6 million a year ago. Net income was USD 496.3 million compared to USD 453.4 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 5.76 compared to USD 5.19 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 5.73 compared to USD 5.16 a year ago.

data.title: Guangdong-Hong Kong Buys AI Firm
Rogers Communications reports third-quarter revenue above estimates
data.description:
(Reuters) -Rogers Communications reported third-quarter revenue above analysts' estimates on Thursday, led by strong growth in its media and sports business as fans tuned in for live games.

The Canadian telecom and media company has poured money into sports properties, including its stake in Maple Leaf Sports & Entertainment, which owns the NBA's Toronto Raptors and the NHL's Toronto Maple Leafs.

Live sports remains a bright spot across the media landscape in both the United States and Canada, with fans flocking to platforms to catch live games despite pricing pressures and shifting global trade dynamics.

Rogers' media revenue grew 26% to C$753 million, helped by higher sports-related revenue from the NHL playoffs and the launch of Warner Bros Discovery's suite of television channels.

It reported quarterly revenue of C$5.35 billion ($3.81 billion), slightly above analysts' average estimate of C$5.32 billion, according to data compiled by LSEG.

($1 = 1.4024 Canadian dollars)

(Reporting by Kritika Lamba in Bengaluru; Editing by Pooja Desai)

Kuehne + Nagel International AG agreed to acquire an additional 24.90% stake in Apex Logistics International (CN) Limited from Partners Group Holding AG.
data.description:
Kuehne + Nagel International AG (SWX:KNIN) agreed to acquire an additional 24.90% stake in Apex Logistics International (CN) Limited from Partners Group Holding AG (SWX:PGHN) on October 23, 2025.

Clifford Chance LLP acted as legal advisor for Partners Group Holding AG. Fangda Partners acted as legal advisor for Partners Group Holding AG. Kpmg International Limited acted as accountant for Partners Group Holding AG.

data.title: Dow beats loss estimates on cost cuts, new US capacity
data.description:
(Reuters) -Chemicals company Dow reported a smaller-than-expected adjusted quarterly loss in the third quarter on Thursday, as cost cuts and higher volumes from new U.S. Gulf Coast assets helped offset weakness in global chemical prices.

Shares of Dow were up 6.5% at $23.10 in premarket trading.

The company, which began a strategic review of some of its European assets in 2024, said it has achieved more than half of its planned $6.5 billion near-term cash support, including $1 billion in capital spending cuts and accelerated delivery of cost reduction targets by end-2026.

CEO Jim Fitterling said Dow's cost discipline and new polyethylene and alkoxylation assets in the U.S. Gulf Coast helped lift margins and volumes in key markets.

Global chemical markets remain under pressure from weak demand, rising production costs, and tightening environmental regulations, particularly in Europe, forcing companies worldwide to reassess strategies and optimize operations.

Dow said addressing regional challenges, including European shutdowns, will deliver an adjusted core profit uplift of nearly $200 million beginning in mid-2026.

The company expects challenging conditions to persist and forecasts fourth-quarter net sales of $9.4 billion, below analysts' forecast of $10.2 billion.

In September, Fitterling had signaled that the company observed stable volumes, strong export capabilities and low-cost positions in the United States during the third quarter.

He added that the polyethylene market is setting up for positive price action following a dip in the second quarter due to tariff uncertainties.

The Michigan-based company reported an adjusted loss of 19 cents per share for the quarter ended September 30, smaller than analysts' average estimate of a loss of 29 cents, according to data compiled by LSEG.

Its quarterly net sales of $9.97 billion, however, fell short of analysts' average forecast of $10.23 billion.

(Reporting by Pooja Menon in Bengaluru; Editing by Tasim Zahid and Krishna Chandra Eluri)

data.title: Emirates Islamic Bank PJSC Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Emirates Islamic Bank PJSC reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported net income was AED 853 million compared to AED 835.24 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was AED 0.157 compared to AED 0.154 a year ago.
For the nine months, net income was AED 2,716.18 million compared to AED 2,498.97 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was AED 0.5 compared to AED 0.46 a year ago.

data.title: Guangzhou Zhujiang Brewery Co., Ltd Reports Earnings Results for the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Guangzhou Zhujiang Brewery Co., Ltd reported earnings results for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the nine months, the company reported sales was CNY 5,073.01 million compared to CNY 4,886.86 million a year ago. Revenue was CNY 5,073.01 million compared to CNY 4,886.86 million a year ago.

Net income was CNY 944.08 million compared to CNY 806.54 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 0.4265 compared to CNY 0.3644 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 0.4265 compared to CNY 0.3644 a year ago.

data.title: India's Hero MotoCorp to enter UK market with MotoGB partnership
data.description:
(Reuters) -Hero MotoCorp, India's largest two-wheeler maker, said on Thursday it will enter the United Kingdom market with a partnership with supplier MotoGB.

(Reporting by Komal Salecha in Bengaluru; Editing by Janane Venkatraman)

data.title: Hebei Broadcasting Wireless Media Co., Ltd. Reports Earnings Results for the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Hebei Broadcasting Wireless Media Co., Ltd. reported earnings results for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the nine months, the company reported sales was CNY 447.96 million compared to CNY 458.03 million a year ago. Revenue was CNY 447.96 million compared to CNY 458.03 million a year ago.

Net income was CNY 217.08 million compared to CNY 189.01 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 0.5427 compared to CNY 0.525 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 0.5427 compared to CNY 0.525 a year ago.

data.title: Rogers posted third quarter revenue above estimates
data.description:
(Reuters) -Rogers Communications reported third-quarter revenue above analysts' estimates on Thursday, as its strategy to promote premium mobile bundles pays off.

The company reported quarterly revenue of C$5.35 billion ($3.81 billion), slightly above analysts' average estimate of C$5.32 billion, according to data compiled by LSEG.

($1 = 1.4024 Canadian dollars)

(Reporting by Kritika Lamba in Bengaluru; Editing by Pooja Desai)
